subtopics.description: What tasks did you work on this week, and what (if anything) changed compared to last week?
coverage_criteria: Tasks worked on this week are identified
Any tasks dropped, added, or shifted in priority vs last week are noted
Time allocation across tasks plausibly accounts for a full work week

subtopics.description: How did you spend your time this week — and did the balance shift from last week?
coverage_criteria: Approximate time share per task type is captured
Any notable shift in time allocation vs last week is noted

Once a usable time split is provided, mark this subtopic complete and move on. Do NOT ask representativeness or catch-all follow-up questions (for example, 'does that breakdown feel representative?' or 'what is not captured there?').

subtopics.description: What tools or methods did you use this week — any new ones, or any you stopped using?
coverage_criteria: Tools used this week are identified
Any tools added or dropped vs last week are noted

subtopics.description: Did you use any AI tools this week — and how did your usage or trust in them compare to last week?
coverage_criteria: AI tool usage (or non-usage) this week is captured
What the AI tool was used for is noted
Any change in how much they trusted or relied on it vs last week is noted

subtopics.description: Who did you work with or for this week — and did that change from last week?
coverage_criteria: Collaborators this week are identified
Any new or dropped collaborations vs last week are noted

subtopics.description: Were there any changes in how you delegated or coordinated work this week?
coverage_criteria: Delegation or coordination patterns this week are described
Any shift in those patterns vs last week is noted

subtopics.description: What was the biggest frustration or inefficiency this week — and is it new or ongoing from last week?
coverage_criteria: Main frustration or pain point this week is identified
Whether it is new or a continuation from last week is noted

subtopics.description: What went well or felt particularly effective this week — anything different from last week?
coverage_criteria: A positive or effective moment this week is identified
Whether it represents a change or improvement vs last week is noted

subtopics.description: Who they depend on and who depends on them
coverage_criteria: Names the roles or teams they interact with most to get their work done.
Describes how work flows to them and from them — who hands off what, and to whom.
For the key dependencies, captures whether the handoff is smooth or a source of friction, and why.

subtopics.description: What frustrates them or slows them down
coverage_criteria: Names specific, recurring frustrations tied to concrete tasks or situations.
For each frustration, captures enough detail to understand what goes wrong, how often, and how much time it costs.
Gets beyond the first answer — probes for frustrations the worker may have normalized or stopped noticing.

subtopics.description: Breadth: all the distinct things they do in a typical week
coverage_criteria: Names the distinct tasks they actually perform — concrete activities, not broad responsibilities or goals.
Make sure to ask for a complete list of tasks, not just the most important ones.
Do NOT keep asking 'what else?' or probe for additional tasks — instead, move on to time allocation or confirm: 'So those are the main things that fill your week?'
Gives a rough sense of where their time goes across these tasks — which ones dominate the week and which are occasional.
